solution:
```java
class Solution {
    public int findTheLongestBalancedSubstring(String s) {
        int n = s.length();
        int[] prefix0 = new int[n + 1];
        int[] prefix1 = new int[n + 1];
        
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            prefix0[i + 1] = prefix0[i] + (s.charAt(i) == '0' ? 1 : 0);
            prefix1[i + 1] = prefix1[i] + (s.charAt(i) == '1' ? 1 : 0);
        }
        
        int maxK = n / 2;
        for (int k = maxK; k >= 1; k--) {
            for (int i = 0; i <= n - 2 * k; i++) {
                int end0 = i + k;
                int start1 = i + k;
                int end1 = i + 2 * k;
                
                int zeros = prefix0[end0] - prefix0[i];
                int ones = prefix1[end1] - prefix1[start1];
                
                if (zeros == k && ones == k) {
                    return 2 * k;
                }
            }
        }
        return 0;
    }
}
```

efficiency: HIGH

time complexity: O(n^2)

space complexity: O(n)

energy implications: LOW

explanation: The solution efficiently finds the longest balanced substring by leveraging prefix sums to quickly compute the number of 0s and 1s in any substring. It iterates over possible lengths of balanced substrings, checking each potential starting point to find the maximum valid length. This approach ensures that we efficiently explore all possible balanced substrings while maintaining clarity and correctness.
